text:
I see the issue — `second` is not available. Let's use `get` to access the second element of the pair instead. We'll use `nth` to extract index 1 (second item) from the pair `[assignee count]`.

Here’s the corrected version using `nth`:

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":"(def in_progress_issues (filter #(= (:state %) "in_progress") (linear_issues)))
(def grouped_by_assignee (group-by :assignee in_progress_issues))
(def assignee_counts (map (fn [a] [a (count (get grouped_by_assignee a))]) (keys grouped_by_assignee)))
(def top_assignee (max-key (fn [pair] (nth pair 1)) assignee_counts))
{:assignee (nth top_assignee 0) :count (nth top_assignee 1)}"}
